channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Oil prices rise higher as Iran denies US talks, dimming deescalation hopes
item.description: Brent crude tops $104 a barrel as hopes fade for deescalation in US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: Malaysia’s prime minister says Iran talks should end war
item.description: Malaysia’s Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said any talks with Iran must aim to end the war.

item.title: Videos show wave of missiles fired from Lebanon to Israel
item.description: Videos show scenes of rocket interceptions in the sky over central Israel.

item.title: Russia slams second US-Israeli strike at Iran’s Bushehr nuclear reactor
item.description: Russia’s foreign ministry has accused the US and Israel of trying to spark a nuclear disaster after a strike at Bushehr.

item.title: Australia bans visitors from Iran amid war in the Middle East
item.description: Australia's Home Affairs Department said decision to ban Iranian visitors amid the war was in the 'national interest'.

item.title: Harrison: ‘There is going to be some kind of a military escalation.’
item.description: Ross Harrison says public talk of diplomacy between the US and Iran is hiding hardline positions.

item.title: Tehran issues warning to regional neighbour if Iranian island occupied
item.description: Iran's parliament speaker issues threat as US administration talks peace while committing more troops to conflict.

item.title: Trump says Iranian leaders want deal ‘so badly’ but fear retaliation
item.description: Donald Trump said Iran is negotiating with the US but is “afraid to say it”.

item.title: Iran war live: US demands Tehran accept defeat; Israel pounds Lebanon
item.description: Iranian official describes US's 15-point plan as 'maximalist' as state television outlines five conditions for peace.

item.title: Egypt says it is ready to host Iran de-escalation talks
item.description: Egypt’s Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty said Cairo is ready to host talks on Iran to support de-escalation.
